reviews.review_description: This really is a very short story that comes as a stage play. The art style is like Pixar ratatouille a bonus! There is no real gameplay but I did enjoy the 25 mins that it was. I did hope for more. Good story, sound and visuals just not much for full price. £2 on sale is spot on and shouldn't leave you with any complaints.

reviews.review_title: Worth the price of a movie tix
reviews.review_description: A considered and beautifully made film. Played with QGO, but if the devs would up the resolution for non techsavvy people, it'll be great

reviews.review_title: it say's in the description a VR Film
reviews.review_description:
Like in every movie not interactive
but sometimes you go to the movie to see it on a flat screen.
Here you are IN the movie and see it happening around you.
and like every good movie with a happy end !
so also the price is the price of a ticket for a movie
please don't cry

Another fantastic immersive animated short film. I loved the interactive elements in the film as well that although simple really gave me an even more immersed feeling of being part of the story. These types of vr experiences have become my favorite way to spend time on my quest 3. This was really well done and I highly recommend it. 🔥🔥❤️❤️✌️

reviews.review_title: The best experience i had in months
reviews.review_description:
Writing 10/10
Soundtrack 10/10
Gameplay (all though little)9/10
Story telling 10/10 
Visuals 9/10 
.
I loved this story and wish it had more content or a sequel.

I had fun with it just a little but it’s barely interactive and just a movie. And you couldn’t save the process, I had to play this twice, I wish it had more twists and turns and multiple  endings. That would be worth like $4.99 and I would kinda like my money back because I thought it would be more interactive more…. detective like more everything.

reviews.review_title: How to return where I left off
reviews.review_description:
I don’t know how to save the sections I have watched when I quit.  I watched  the first 20 minutes and had to quit.  The next day I tried to resume where I left off, but had to start from the beginning again. 
HELP!

developer_response.body: Dear Cww922, we apologies if you are encountering issues with our VR experience. If you look closely in the startup menu, you can see 6 buttons right below the movie projector and choose what part you would like to continue with. We hope you enjoy the rest of the experience and are here if you need anything else.

reviews.review_title: The bugs take away from the experience
reviews.review_description:
I enjoyed the story and the interactions but the experience was ruined due to the performance issues (jittering) one scene refused to proceed to the next and since there is no way of going back to the main menu I was forced to quit the game meaning starting at the beginning again with no way to skip at this point I had lost interest.

Surprised that there are no subtitles either, it’s a shame because these bugs were reported almost a year ago by other users but they are still present.

I don’t like leaving a bad review as a lot of work goes into these but this wasn’t as enjoyable as it should have been and it’s one of those which if the bugs get fixed tomorrow your not really going to go back and revisit.

developer_response.body:
Hello OculusBosh, 
We are trully sorry to learn that you encountered some technical issue with Madrid Noir. We are working on making the experience as smooth as possible, and we hope that you will change your mind and retry the game soon.
Best, 
The Madrid Noir Team

reviews.review_description:
Really nice artwork and lovely story with some great voice-acting. 
But some scenes could be glitchy with some  stuttering. Also objects would fall through other objects when you put them back down again.
But overall it was a nice VR experience.

It was a fun VR story.  Definitely worth buying if it's on sale.  But personally, I didn't think it made effective use of VR, i.e. it could have been told almost just as well in 2D.  I know the effective use of VR is always a challenge, but I like to see more effort.
If you like interactive mini movie experiences on oculus this is a great gem.

A beautifully crafted theatrical world with an emotional and powerful story behind it.

This is a 45 minute narrative experience with enough light interaction to make the player feel a part of a mystery that unravels right in front of you.

The artwork, animation and lighting push the limitations of the quest and in my opinion, this has set a new bar for narrative storytelling in VR.

developer_response.body:
Hi Skezney_Hawkes,

Thank  you so much for your review, we are so happy to read that it resonated with you. 

Warmth,
The Madrid Noir Team

reviews.review_description: I usually don't leave reviews, but I was curious about this one. And honestly, it is really well made. The story, the animation, the way you interact with the story. It's for all ages, will definietely be sharing with my family

reviews.review_title: Great experience in English and Spanish!
reviews.review_description:
TLDR: Give it a try, excellent art and atmosphere, good story. It doesn't have subtitles but has a great voice acting both in English and Spanish. Great developer worth supporting.

My review:

This VR experience is like a short film with interactive sequences where you can grab some things and interact with the environment a bit, but that is not the main point of this experience, this is just a way of getting you more immersed in the story, and it works!

 The strengths of this experience are clearly the style, the story, and the voice acting.

The style is beautiful, a cartoonish design with a way of showing the story as if you were watching a theatrical act that works amazingly well, it also has great environment decoration that shows a conflictive Spain with a civil war waiting around the corner. 
The story is okay, I thought that the Spanish conflict was going to be more relevant but is not, it's more family friendly and wholesome, I still really enjoyed it.
I really liked the voice acting, although it has no subtitles, it's dubbed in English, Spanish and French. English is the OV and you can notice that in a better lip sync, but I also watched it in Spanish as I am a native speaker and it's good too, definitely more immersive even if the English version is more polished.

I watched it twice and in my first time I encountered some minor bugs with the physics and a bit of frame drop at the beginning but it got better quickly and it was nothing game breaking. The second time it was all smooth.

If you are thinking of buying it, just do it, I believe is definitely worth the price, take in mind this is not a AAA title, it's a little indie studio that did an amazing job!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your kind words and honest review! We are indeed a small team and so passionate about story telling and animation in VR, so it's truly a pleasure  that this content resonates with you. 
Warmth, 
The Madrid Noir team

reviews.review_description:
I have to say, the story was amazing. The plot, the background was a great story. But there was little to no interaction. I only was able to interact around 3 times. The story ended very fast and it was left on a cliff hanger. 
A little buggy too.

developer_response.body:
Hi bankrupts,

Thank you for your review, that's wonderful to read that you enjoyed the story, even if the cliff hanger left you wondering. You're right, the experience is more a storytelling one than a game, the interaction is light. Sorry if there was any confusion about this when you added the app to your library. We did a patch update to smooth out the bugs and invite you to dive in again, with the perspective that it's an immersive narrative rather than a game. Thank you again for your feedback. 

Warmth,
The Madrid Noir Team

reviews.review_description: I like the work a lot but there is a bug in the sequence where I am supposed to hang the photos. I am stuck and can not go further in the experience. Unfortunately I did not receive an answer of the developper-team. I contaceted them via there website. What to do?

It needs less storytelling and more interactivity. Decent visuals, B. C- use of 360. C- use of interactivity. Why all that clues if they will not be used? D- there. No mystery to solve in a film noir game seems like a fail, but D- because there is a bit of a dull story.  Definitely not worth more than a couple dollars.

developer_response.body:
Hi langbulang,

Thank you for your feedback. 
We are sorry to hear Madrid Noir didn't fit your expectations. You are indeed correct that this is much more of a storytelling experience than a game. 

Warmth,
The Madrid Noir Team

reviews.review_description: I’d be fine with less interaction if the story were at all interesting. Cute dog though.

developer_response.body:
Hi priceliketag,

Thank you for you feedback, sorry to hear the story didn't resonate with you. 
We like Paquita the pug too ;)

Warmth,
The Madrid Noir Team

reviews.review_title: A great animated movie in VR !
reviews.review_description:
Madrid Noir is not a game but a wonderful short movie.
The animations of the little girl and the cute little dog are beautiful, Graphics are charming and story is great !

First of all, as a madrilenian, I tell you that It's amazing how well it's done and how recognizable the locations are. It's an interactive story and as one of my first ones I've found it endearing. If you are looking for a game this isn't  your option but  you should know you miss a trip to Madrid and a story made with LOVE that show on the details.

developer_response.body:
Hi MayorMenta,

Thank you so much for your review, we really appreciate it! So glad to hear you enjoyed Madrid Noir as much as we do ;)

Warmth,
The Madrid Noir Team
